name: Spiced Fish - Chekiang
recipeIngredient: 1 tbsp Rice wine or dry sherry
recipeInstructions:
1. Select firm fish fillets and cut them into pieces approximately 1/2 inch thick by 1 inch by 3 inches.
2. Marinate the fish for 2 to 3 hours in a mixture of ginger root, green onions, soy sauce, and rice wine or dry sherry.
3. Heat oil in a heavy skillet over medium heat.
4. Drain the fish slices, reserving the marinade, and fry until brown on both sides. Remove from the skillet and drain on paper towels.
5. Turn off the heat under the skillet and combine five-spice powder, sugar, and boiling water with the reserved marinade in a bowl.
6. Return the fish to the skillet with the sauce and cook for 1-2 minutes until the sauce is reduced by half.
7. Serve at room temperature.
(Note: This recipe is from "The Regional Cooking of China" by Margret Gin and Alfred E. Castle, published by 101 Productions in San Francisco in 1975.)
